rulings.Case Title: ROBERT SHALBERG VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 7, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Tosco Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed October 6. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DOLYE D GREEN et al VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 7, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production under Aguilar. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Green¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant (joint compound) was a substantial factor in causing his mesothelioma pursuant to Texas law, including Borg-Warner v. Flores (Texas 2007) 232 S.W.3d 765; Georgia-Pacific Corp. v. Stephens (Tex. App. 2007) 239 S.W.3d 304; Georgia-Pacific Corp. v. Bostic (Tex. App. 2010) 320 S.W.3d 588; Smith v. Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc. (Tex. App. 2010) 307 S.W.3d 829. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DOLYE D GREEN et al VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 7, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Terex Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production under Aguilar and plaintiffs' late evidence will not be considered by the court, thus plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Green was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor under Texas law. Defendant's untimeliness objections to the opposition are sustained. Pursuant to the court's November 22 order staggering the hearings on summary motions in this case, plaintiffs' opposition was due to be filed and served by November 30. According to Lexis, plaintiffs did not file their opposition until December 6 (it was electronically filed after close of business on December 5), one day before the hearing. (See CRC 3.1300.) Further, a courtesy copy of the opposition was not delivered to the court until just before 5:00pm on December 5 and that copy does not include a proof of service demonstrating that it was ever properly and timely served on defendant. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) ...(To be continued)
